question_text: A student drags a box across a carpet at a steady speed. Which of the following is true?

options.label: A
options.text: The pulling force is greater than the friction force

options.label: B
options.text: The pulling force equals the friction force

options.label: C
options.text: The friction force is greater than the pulling force

options.label: D
options.text: There is no friction force because the box is moving

explanation: Moving at steady speed means constant velocity, which requires balanced forces. The forward pulling force must equal the backward friction force so that the net force is zero and there is no acceleration.
